defining_markers.evidence: atlas paper, MHC class II component
atlas paper, MHC class II component

shared_macrophage_markers.evidence: atlas paper, coagulation factor XIII A chain

negative_markers.evidence: atlas paper, lacks LC marker langerin
atlas paper, lacks LC marker CD1a

location.co_localization: Predicted to co-locate with endothelial cells in neurovascular microenvironments

functions.angiogenesis: Gene ontology analysis showed MHCII+ macrophages express gene programs that drive angiogenesis (Supplementary Tables 23-26)
functions.antigen_presentation: High expression of MHC class II genes suggests capacity for antigen presentation, though Suo et al. (2022) noted that antigen presentation may not be a key function of macrophages during early gestation, with MHCII upregulation occurring between 10-12 PCW coinciding with adaptive lymphocyte expansion
functions.scarless_healing: Part of the macrophage compartment implicated in contributing to scarless healing properties of prenatal skin

developmental_context.gestational_timing: MHCII hi macrophage populations enriched in later stages of gestation (Suo et al., 2022)
developmental_context.immune_effector_acquisition: Upregulated genes encoding immune effector functions in later gestation
developmental_context.annotation_source: Macrophage subsets annotated using marker genes from Suo et al. (2022)

evidence_quotes.text: Gene ontology analysis showed that the four macrophage subsets (LYVE1+, MHCII+, TML and iron-recycling) expressed gene programs that drive angiogenesis
acquisition of macrophage antigen-presentation properties (e.g., MHCII upregulation) between 10 and 12 pcw coincided with the expansion of adaptive lymphocytes
evidence_quotes.section: Heterogeneity of prenatal myeloid cells across organs and gestation

evidence_quotes.text: a large fraction of neighborhoods within the iron-recycling and MHCII hi macrophage populations were enriched in later stages of gestation. We found that these subpopulations upregulated genes encoding for immune effector functions
evidence_quotes.section: Heterogeneity of prenatal myeloid cells across organs and gestation
